summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Cloud Code functions execute without timeout enforcement, allowing infinite loops to block the Node.js event loop and cause denial of service.
expected_security_impact: Cloud Code function with infinite loop should timeout after reasonable duration (e.g., 30 seconds) and server should remain responsive to other requests.
actual_observed_result: Cloud Code function containing infinite loop executed without timeout, blocking the Node.js event loop. Server became unresponsive to health checks and subsequent requests, demonstrating complete denial of service.
verification_method: Deploy Cloud Code with infinite loop function, call function via authenticated request, then test server responsiveness via health endpoint and normal function calls. If server becomes unresponsive, DoS vulnerability is confirmed.
evidence_produced_by_run: Cloud Code function containing while(true) infinite loop deployed successfully
Function call did not timeout after 5 seconds
Server health check timed out after function execution
Subsequent normal function calls timed out
Container logs showed function running indefinitely without termination
Server required container restart to recover
limitations_or_blockers: Infinite loop was manually terminated by curl timeout, not server-side timeout
DoS duration limited by PoC timeout to prevent indefinite hang

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones target repository and adds malicious Cloud Code file with infinite loop function
